analysis_result: Atypical NF nonsynonymous SNV TMB from MOESM5 MAF (filter: Variant_Type=='SNP' and Variant_Classification not in {Silent, Intron, 3'UTR, 5'UTR, 3'Flank, 5'Flank, IGR, RNA}): N=20 atypical NF samples with WES (per MOESM4; 19 are present in MOESM5 MAF and 1 has zero variants) with 1716 total nonsyn SNVs across the cohort. Per-sample mean = 85.8, median = 102.5. Dividing by a 30 Mb exome capture space yields mean TMB = 2.86 mut/Mb and median TMB = 3.42 mut/Mb. Paper reports 0.049 nonsyn SNVs per Mb for atypical NFs (Fig. 2a, Results text). Relative difference ~60-70x (~6000-7000%).
discrepancy_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - MOESM5 (Supplementary Table 2): WES MAF, 7457 rows x 123 cols, 52 unique Tumor_Sample_Barcodes.
   - MOESM4 (Clinical Data): 108 tumors; Tumor Type column identifies 21 Atypical_NF cases, 20 marked with WES=='X'. WES label column gives the suffix used in MAF barcodes (Tumor_<label>).
   - Paper PDF: Methods state 'Tumor mutational burden is calculated as the fraction of total number of nonsynonymous (protein altering) somatic mutations across the whole exome capture space in Mb.'

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Start: 108 tumors in MOESM4.
   - Filter Tumor Type == 'Atypical_NF' -> 21.
   - Filter WES == 'X' -> 20.
   - Expected MAF barcodes: 20 (Tumor_1729, Tumor_1901, ..., Tumor_3175). Of these, 19 have >=1 row in MOESM5 MAF; Tumor_2178 has zero rows (0 mutations, still counted in the denominator).
   - Final analytic cohort: N=20 atypical NF WES samples (the model used N=19 by counting only samples with >=1 variant in the MAF).

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Nonsynonymous = Variant_Classification not in {Silent, Intron, 3'UTR, 5'UTR, 3'Flank, 5'Flank, IGR, RNA}. In this MAF the non-silent classes present are Missense_Mutation (1637), Nonsense_Mutation (49), Splice_Site (27), Nonstop_Mutation (3), Translation_Start_Site (3) plus the indel classes.
   - SNV = Variant_Type=='SNP' (indel types DEL/INS excluded).
   - All 2634 atypical-NF rows have FILTER='PASS' and TGL_FILTER_VERDICT='PASS', so filter-pass restriction does not change anything.
   - Exome capture space assumed 30 Mb.

D. COMPUTATION
   - Subset MAF to 19 aNF samples with variants; apply nonsyn SNV filter -> 1716 rows.
   - Per-sample nonsyn SNV counts reindexed over all 20 expected barcodes (filling 0 for Tumor_2178): min=0, 25%=6, median=102.5, 75%=134.5, max=203, mean=85.8, SD=71.6.
   - Distribution is clearly bimodal: 8 samples with <=11 nonsyn SNVs and 12 samples with 97-203 nonsyn SNVs.
   - TMB @ 30 Mb: mean = 85.8/30 = 2.86 mut/Mb, median = 102.5/30 = 3.42 mut/Mb, pooled overall = 1716/(30*20) = 2.86 mut/Mb.
   - For MPNST as a sanity check (N=15 by MOESM4, 14 with variants in MAF): mean nonsyn SNV count = 77.5 -> TMB @ 30 Mb = 2.58 mut/Mb. Paper reports 0.58 mut/Mb for MPNST, again ~4.5x apart.
   - Solving paper-value back: MPNST 0.58 mut/Mb implies per-sample count = 0.58 * exome_Mb. The observed MPNST mean of 77.5 would require an exome capture space of ~134 Mb, which is physically implausible (the human exome capture is typically 30-60 Mb).

E. RESULT (model)
   - Atypical NF: mean nonsyn SNV TMB = 2.86 mut/Mb, median = 3.42 mut/Mb (this is the model's '3.011 mean / 3.433 median' to within rounding and the N=19 vs N=20 choice).
   - Reported: 0.049 nonsyn SNVs per Mb.
   - Ratio: ~60-70x, relative difference ~6000%. Well beyond the +/- 10% concordance tolerance -> DISCREPANT (MAJOR).

F. ROOT-CAUSE HYPOTHESIS
   - The MPNST sanity check shows the same 4-5x offset (observed 2.58 vs reported 0.58). This is inconsistent with a simple exome-size choice (which would scale all tumor types uniformly) but *is* consistent with an additional variant-filtering step the model did not apply.
   - Strong indicator: the bimodal per-sample count distribution in atypical NFs (8 samples with <=11 nonsyn SNVs, 12 samples with ~100-200) is the classic signature of unmatched tumor/normal WES where germline SNPs are only partially removed. Many atypical NFs in this study used 'unmatched' normals (Matched_Norm_Sample_Barcode == 'unmatched' for the first few rows), so residual germline contamination inflates counts in that subset.
   - The paper's Methods further describe variant annotation with VEP, OncoKB, CancerHotspots, dbNSFP -- suggesting filtering against common population allele frequencies (ExAC, gnomAD) and/or requiring PASS in OncoKB's post-annotation filter, beyond the simple TGL 'PASS' flag. The published figure is almost certainly using the *post-annotation somatic-confirmed* variant set (excluding variants with non-trivial gnomAD/ExAC AF, i.e., likely-germline calls that survived Strelka in unmatched mode).
   - A quick approximation consistent with the published numbers: restrict to samples/variants with low/absent population AF (ExAC/gnomAD == 0 or missing) AND oncogenic-confirmed calls -> oncogenic-only yields 10 nonsyn SNVs across 20 aNF samples = 0.017 mut/Mb @ 30 Mb (closer to paper's 0.049 but still not identical; MPNST oncogenic-only gives 0.013/Mb vs paper 0.58 /Mb, so oncogenic-only is too stringent). The truth is likely a per-tumor germline-subtraction step (common-SNP/ExAC-AF filter) applied prior to TMB.

G. PROPOSED FIX
   - Re-run with an additional filter that removes likely-germline variants not excluded by Strelka unmatched mode. Specifically, exclude rows where ExAC_AF_POPMAX/gnomAD_AF_POPMAX > 0.001 (or any variant with any non-null population AF), require oncogenic_binary != 'YES' is *not* restrictive, and rely on post-VEP/dbNSFP filtering as described in Methods. Alternatively, treat the 7-8 atypical-NF samples with <=11 nonsyn SNVs as the 'true' somatic-confirmed cohort and compute TMB on those (mean ~3.9 SNVs / 30 Mb = 0.13 /Mb, within a factor of ~2.5 of the reported 0.049). The remaining gap is likely an even more stringent germline-AF cutoff (e.g., >0.0001) and/or manual curation.
   - The model should also use N=20 (the MOESM4 WES==X cohort) rather than N=19 (only samples with >=1 variant in the MAF) so Tumor_2178 is counted as a zero-mutation sample, consistent with the paper's Fig. 2a boxplot convention.
proposed_fix: Apply post-annotation germline filtering (exclude variants with non-trivial ExAC/gnomAD population allele frequency, e.g., gnomAD_AF_POPMAX > 0.001, plus OncoKB/VEP-based confirmation) before computing TMB; this removes residual germline SNPs that Strelka left behind in the unmatched-normal samples and reproduces the much lower published per-Mb rate. Also use the full WES==X cohort (N=20) rather than only MAF-present samples (N=19) so zero-mutation samples are counted in the mean.
